text: 'Never stale by construction' overclaims — dashboard eliminates cache drift but status entries can still be stale
Auto-log content is low-signal (prefix truncation, no diff, no rationale) and pollutes memory_query and memory_list preview window
Auto-log should be in same transaction as status write — partial success and race window on previous status read
Lifecycle tag vocabulary drift — plan introduces new vocabulary conflicting with current tool descriptions (paused vs maintenance, done vs completed)
Soft validation skips entirely when tags are omitted (tags is optional in API contract)
decisions/* excluded from dashboard query despite draft claiming cross-cutting support
SQL fetches full content for all status entries but only needs first N chars — use substr() server-side
'No migration needed' is misleading — data cleanup and convention updates are real migration work even without schema changes
Hybrid model (Alternative B): computed core + curated notes overlay is better than fully eliminating the curated layer
Discoverability regression for partially-initialized projects (autoAddToWorkbench fired on any first entry, dashboard requires status key)
Write-path enforcement (Alternative C) — lifecycle tags should be enforced, not just warned, if they're the primary dashboard signal
Response size scales linearly with tracked namespaces — orient is mandatory START HERE call, payload size matters
Plan removes 'read before write' safety without adding server-side overwrite protection — blind stale overwrites remain the central risk
workbench-notes reintroduces manual state and staleness risk — must be explicitly non-authoritative and tightly scoped
Canonicalization semantics: paused->maintenance is not obviously equivalent, need explicit mapping + warning on normalization
'Session window' for missing-log detection is not a well-defined primitive in stateless HTTP transport
